# Mantis Registry Expansion — Proposal Report

*Generated by the `mantis-registry-research` agent on 2026-04-21.*

Three schema-compliant drafts land in this directory alongside this report:

- `dockerfile-hadolint.json` — 25 M1 / 38 M7 / 2 security-defer (65 total, `phase_2_gap: true`)
- `yaml-yamllint.json` — 6 M1 / 7 M7 / 0 security-defer (13 total)
- `github-actions-actionlint.json` — 16 M1 / 2 M7 / 3 security-defer (21 total)

All three pass the schema contract: `schema_version: "1.0"`, every required `categories` key present, `never_mapped: true` on `security_defer_to_reaper`, `coverage_stats` populated. Validated via `json.load` + key-shape inspection.

## Proposals

Ranked by (value to Mantis users) × (integration ease):

1. **hadolint** (Dockerfile) — [https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint](https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint). ~70 DL rules plus ShellCheck pass-through. JSON output confirmed. v2.14.0 Sep 2025, active. ~36% correctness_m1 / ~48% idiom_m7 / ~3% security-defer. No M5 witness surface (Dockerfile build is out of zero-dep scope). Clean taxonomy — DL rules are posture signals, not CWE claims. **Drafted.**
2. **yamllint** (YAML) — [https://github.com/adrienverge/yamllint](https://github.com/adrienverge/yamllint). 23 rules, v1.38.0 Jan 2026. `parsable` format is regex-parseable via stdlib. ~26% correctness_m1, zero security rules (empty defer bucket by design). M5 could witness key-duplicate collapse via `yaml.safe_load` returning a shrunken dict. **Drafted.**
3. **actionlint** (GitHub Actions) — [https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint](https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint). 22 check kinds, JSON emit, v1.7.12 Mar 2026. ~73% correctness_m1, ~14% security-defer (script injection, hardcoded credentials, permissions). Fills the CI workflow gap. **Drafted.**
4. **pylint** — [https://pylint.readthedocs.io/](https://pylint.readthedocs.io/). ~400 messages, JSON emit. Overlaps with existing ruff map; value is the ~150 non-overlapping messages (design smells, refactor suggestions). Medium dedupe cost against `languages/python.json`.
5. **ESLint** — [https://eslint.org/](https://eslint.org/). ~280 core rules + huge plugin ecosystem, JSON emit. Overlaps with biome; unique value is the plugin coverage (react, vue, node, import, jsx-a11y).
6. **SwiftLint** — [https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint](https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint). 200+ rules, JSON reporter, v0.63.2 Jan 2026. Fills the iOS mobile gap. Low integration cost.
7. **detekt** (Kotlin) — [https://github.com/detekt/detekt](https://github.com/detekt/detekt). ~250 rules across 10 categories. SARIF (JSON-shaped). v1.23.8 Feb 2025 — **maintenance marginal**, flag as risk.
8. **PMD** (Java + Apex + 16 languages) — [https://github.com/pmd/pmd](https://github.com/pmd/pmd). 400+ rules, JSON emit, v7.23.0 Mar 2026. Overlaps spotbugs; unique value is Apex + the multi-lang coverage.
9. **SQLFluff** — [https://github.com/sqlfluff/sqlfluff](https://github.com/sqlfluff/sqlfluff). ~50 rules (aliasing, layout, references, structure). JSON emit, v4.1.0 Mar 2026. No security rules. Clean fit.
10. **stylelint** (CSS) — [https://github.com/stylelint/stylelint](https://github.com/stylelint/stylelint). 100+ rules, JSON emit, v17.8.0 Apr 2026. Low-priority but cheap; mostly idiom_m7.
11. **tflint** (Terraform) — [https://github.com/terraform-linters/tflint](https://github.com/terraform-linters/tflint). ~200 rules across core + AWS/Azure/GCP rulesets. JSON emit, v0.62.0 Apr 2026. IaC security rules defer to Reaper.
12. **ktlint** (Kotlin) — [https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint). ~80 rules, JSON reporter, v1.8.0 Nov 2025. Overlaps detekt if both shipped.
13. **Spectral** (OpenAPI / AsyncAPI) — [https://github.com/stoplightio/spectral](https://github.com/stoplightio/spectral). JSON emit, v6.15.0 Apr 2025 — **maintenance marginal**, flag as risk.
14. **Pyright** — [https://github.com/microsoft/pyright](https://github.com/microsoft/pyright). Type checker complement to mypy. JSON emit. Defer to Phase 2 if ruff/mypy coverage is already sufficient.
15. **actionlint extensions (reusable workflow lints), cppcheck, Dart analyzer, Infer** — honorable mentions, Phase 3.

## Drafted rule-maps

- [shared/rules/proposed/dockerfile-hadolint.json](dockerfile-hadolint.json)
- [shared/rules/proposed/yaml-yamllint.json](yaml-yamllint.json)
- [shared/rules/proposed/github-actions-actionlint.json](github-actions-actionlint.json)

## Schema gaps

Migration proposals only — **no silent bucket additions**:

1. **Pass-through linters.** hadolint embeds ShellCheck; actionlint embeds pyflakes/shellcheck. The current schema has no `cross_references` field to declare "rules matching `SC*` route via `languages/shell.json`." Handled in prose via top-level `note` fields today. *Proposal: optional `cross_references: [{pattern, delegate_to}]` array in schema v1.1 — non-breaking.*

2. **Posture vs. security.** hadolint `DL3002` (final user is root) and `DL3004` (sudo use) are adjacent to CWE-250/269 but are posture signals, not concrete CWE claims. Routing to `security_defer_to_reaper` is the safe Phase-1 call — Reaper owns the lane and can decide whether to surface or ignore. *Proposal: consider a 7th `posture_defer_to_reaper` bucket in v1.1 ONLY if Reaper grows a distinct posture surface. Do not unilaterally add.*

3. **Config-style cops** (yamllint `line-length`, `indentation`) don't fit neatly into `idiom_m7` vs `complexity_m7`. Current draft routes them to `idiom_m7` — the spirit matches but the definition could be tightened in schema v1.1 to accept a `style` sub-bucket keyed off the rubric axis it influences.

## Non-duplication audit

Every security-framed rule across the three drafts routes to `security_defer_to_reaper` with `never_mapped: true`. No leaks into M1/M7.

| Registry | Rule ID | Family | Reaper CWE |
|---|---|---|---|
| hadolint | `DL3002` | Final user is root | CWE-250 / CWE-269 |
| hadolint | `DL3004` | sudo use in Dockerfile | CWE-250 |
| yamllint | *(none)* | — | empty by design |
| actionlint | `credentials` | Hardcoded secrets in workflow | CWE-798 |
| actionlint | `script-injection` | Untrusted input in `run:` step | CWE-77 / CWE-78 |
| actionlint | `permissions` | Over-broad `GITHUB_TOKEN` permissions | CWE-250 |

Reaper R3 non-duplication contract upheld across all three drafts.
